spec_match.rationale: Exercises a real surface of the spec's headline behaviour — Manifest update eligibility algorithm formally specified; icon urls treated as Cache-Control: immutable…

interactive.rationale: 1 buttons / 2 inputs / 1 selects / 0 ranges driving live JS — well past the static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept page lede restates the feature-index blurb's key promise (overlap on chrome, eligibility, icon, immutable).

edge_cases.rationale: Demo runs the happy path; no visible feature detection, error handling, or no-support fallback in the script.

references.rationale: Page includes the chromestatus.com/feature/5148463647686656 link in the see-also section.

design_system.rationale: All colours come from the shared CSS variables and the layout follows the stage + controls editorial pattern.

openQuestions.title: Cover at least one realistic failure mode
openQuestions.detail: Add feature detection at the top of the script and surface the result. Handle the permission-denied / API-rejection case in the catch branch with a visible message. If the feature is flag-gated or origin-trial, show explicitly what the page does when the API is absent.

summary: manifest-update-rules under web-app-manifest-specify-update-eligibility-icon-urls-are-cache-control-immutabl: 5 pass / 1 partial / 0 fail. Strong interactive surface, design system clean, spec story complete.
